manifest.description: VibeOps built-in audit rules: OWASP-aligned vulnerability checks, vibe-code anti-patterns, and known-vulnerable npm packages.

rules.title: Use of eval() in source
rules.description: eval() runs arbitrary code from a string. Classic injection sink.
rules.recommendation: Replace with JSON.parse, a function table, or a parser.

rules.title: Dynamic Function() constructor
rules.description: new Function('...') is equivalent to eval and runs attacker-controlled code.
rules.recommendation: Refactor to a static function. Never pass untrusted input.

rules.title: dangerouslySetInnerHTML usage
rules.description: Bypasses Reacts XSS protection. Risky if HTML originates from user input or untrusted API.
rules.recommendation: Render via JSX, or sanitize HTML with DOMPurify before inserting.

rules.title: document.write() in source
rules.description: document.write injects HTML into the live DOM and is a common XSS vector.
rules.recommendation: Use createElement/textContent or framework rendering.

rules.title: Direct innerHTML assignment
rules.description: innerHTML with concatenated values is a classic XSS sink.
rules.recommendation: Use textContent for text, or sanitize HTML through DOMPurify.

rules.title: Shell command built from template literal
rules.description: child_process.exec / execSync with a template literal is a command injection sink if any interpolated value is user-controlled.
rules.recommendation: Use execFile / spawn with an argv array. Never let user input become part of the shell string.

rules.title: spawn() called with shell:true
rules.description: shell:true reintroduces shell parsing and command-injection risk.
rules.recommendation: Drop shell:true and pass arguments as an array.

rules.title: Possible SQL string concatenation
rules.description: Building SQL by concatenating user-supplied values is the canonical SQL-injection pattern.
rules.recommendation: Use parameterized queries (?, $1) or a query builder that escapes values.

rules.title: MD5 used for hashing
rules.description: MD5 is cryptographically broken and unsuitable for password hashing or signature verification.
rules.recommendation: Use SHA-256 for digests, bcrypt/argon2/scrypt for passwords, HMAC-SHA256 for message auth.

rules.title: SHA-1 used for hashing
rules.description: SHA-1 is deprecated for cryptographic use due to collision attacks.
rules.recommendation: Switch to SHA-256 or SHA-3.

rules.title: Math.random() near token/secret/password
rules.description: Math.random() is not cryptographically secure. Tokens generated this way are predictable.
rules.recommendation: Use crypto.randomUUID() or crypto.randomBytes() for security-sensitive randomness.

rules.title: JWT none algorithm allowed
rules.description: alg:'none' lets attackers forge tokens without a signature.
rules.recommendation: Pin the algorithm (HS256/RS256) on verification and reject 'none'.

rules.title: CORS Access-Control-Allow-Origin wildcard
rules.description: Wildcard CORS combined with credentialed requests exposes authenticated APIs to any origin.
rules.recommendation: List allowed origins explicitly. Never combine wildcard with credentials.

rules.title: Express CORS configured with origin:true
rules.description: origin:true reflects any callers Origin and effectively allows all origins.
rules.recommendation: Pass an explicit origin allow-list or a function checking against one.

rules.title: TLS certificate verification disabled
rules.description: rejectUnauthorized:false disables certificate validation, allowing trivial MITM attacks.
rules.recommendation: Remove the flag, install correct CA certs, or pin via ca option.

rules.title: NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED=0
rules.description: Globally disables TLS verification across the Node process.
rules.recommendation: Remove the override, fix the underlying cert chain.

rules.title: Private key block in source file
rules.description: PEM-encoded private key found in source. Anyone with repo access has the key.
rules.recommendation: Rotate immediately. Move keys to a secret store. Audit git history.

rules.title: Hardcoded AWS access key id
rules.description: AKIA/ASIA-prefixed strings match AWS credential format.
rules.recommendation: Rotate IAM credentials, remove from source, store in env/secret manager, audit CloudTrail.

rules.title: Hardcoded Stripe live secret key
rules.description: sk_live_ keys are production Stripe credentials granting full account access.
rules.recommendation: Rotate immediately in Stripe dashboard. Move to env vars. Audit usage.

rules.title: Hardcoded Google API key
rules.description: AIza-prefixed Google API keys checked into code can incur billing or grant data access.
rules.recommendation: Restrict by HTTP referrer/IP, rotate, move to env vars.

rules.title: Hardcoded Slack webhook URL
rules.description: Slack incoming webhook URLs are bearer credentials.
rules.recommendation: Rotate the webhook in Slack, store in env var, restrict to server-only code.

rules.title: Supabase service-role key in client-side code
rules.description: SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY bypasses RLS. Referencing in client-bundled code exposes full DB access.
rules.recommendation: Restrict to server routes / edge functions. Use anon key in client code.

rules.title: Auth token stored in localStorage
rules.description: Tokens in localStorage are accessible to any script (including XSS payloads).
rules.recommendation: Store auth tokens in HttpOnly Secure cookies; use SameSite=Lax/Strict.

rules.title: Possible open redirect from query parameter
rules.description: Redirecting to a URL from query params without an allow-list lets attackers craft phishing links.
rules.recommendation: Validate redirect targets against an allow-list of paths or hosts.

rules.title: Object merge from untrusted body
rules.description: Merging user JSON into an empty object without cleaning __proto__/constructor allows prototype pollution.
rules.recommendation: Use Object.create(null), or schema-validate (zod/yup) before merge.

rules.title: CSRF protection disabled
rules.description: Disabling csrf middleware on state-changing endpoints exposes the app to CSRF.
rules.recommendation: Re-enable CSRF tokens, or require same-origin + SameSite cookies + custom header.

rules.title: Helmet contentSecurityPolicy disabled
rules.description: Disabling CSP removes a major XSS mitigation.
rules.recommendation: Configure CSP with explicit script-src/style-src; use nonces or hashes.

rules.title: DEBUG=True in committed env
rules.description: Production debug modes leak stack traces, env vars, and source paths.
rules.recommendation: Drive debug from environment, not committed config. Default to false.

rules.title: IAM policy with wildcard Action and Resource
rules.description: Wildcard actions on wildcard resources grant admin-equivalent privilege.
rules.recommendation: Scope Action and Resource to the minimum required.

rules.title: Security-flagged TODO/FIXME left in code
rules.description: TODOs mentioning security/auth/token are common abandonment markers for unfinished hardening.
rules.recommendation: Convert to a tracked task and resolve, or delete if no longer relevant.

rules.title: console.log left in non-test source
rules.description: AI-generated debug prints frequently survive into committed code.
rules.recommendation: Remove or replace with a leveled logger (pino, winston).

rules.title: @ts-ignore directive present
rules.description: @ts-ignore disables type checking for the next line and tends to hide real bugs.
rules.recommendation: Replace with a typed assertion or fix the underlying type. Prefer @ts-expect-error.

rules.title: Explicit any type
rules.description: Frequent any annotations defeat the point of TypeScript.
rules.recommendation: Replace with proper types, generics, or unknown plus a narrowing check.

rules.title: Unfinished TODO/FIXME marker
rules.description: AI assistants often emit TODO placeholders for logic they did not implement.
rules.recommendation: Convert to a tracked task; delete if no longer relevant.

rules.title: Empty catch block
rules.description: Swallowing errors silently is a top source of invisible production bugs.
rules.recommendation: At minimum log the error. Better: rethrow, surface to user, or handle a specific case.

rules.title: Empty finally block
rules.description: Empty finally blocks are usually leftover scaffolding.
rules.recommendation: Delete the block, or add the cleanup it was meant to do.

rules.title: README.md missing
rules.description: No top-level README. Future maintainers and AI assistants lack onboarding context.
rules.recommendation: Add a README: what it does, how to run, env vars, deploy target.

rules.title: LICENSE file missing
rules.description: Without a license, default is all-rights-reserved; collaborators have no legal use right.
rules.recommendation: Add a LICENSE file (MIT, Apache-2.0, or proprietary).

rules.title: .gitignore missing
rules.description: Without .gitignore, .env secrets, build outputs, and node_modules can be committed.
rules.recommendation: Add .gitignore with: node_modules, .env*, dist, build, out, .DS_Store.

rules.title: .env present but no .env.example
rules.description: Onboarding teammates need to know required env vars without seeing real values.
rules.recommendation: Create .env.example listing every required variable with placeholders.

rules.title: Dockerfile runs as root
rules.description: Containers without USER directives run as root, increasing blast radius if compromised.
rules.recommendation: Add USER node before CMD. Set ownership on copied files.

rules.title: ESLint v8 has reached end-of-life
rules.description: ESLint 8.x stopped receiving security fixes. v9+ uses flat config.
rules.recommendation: Migrate to ESLint 9 with eslint.config.js flat config.

rules.title: Vulnerable axios version (SSRF + credential leak)
rules.description: axios <1.7.4 is vulnerable to SSRF and credential leakage via path traversal.
rules.recommendation: Upgrade to axios >=1.7.4.

rules.title: Next.js below cache poisoning fix
rules.description: Next.js <14.2.10 contains a cache-poisoning issue that can serve attacker content.
rules.recommendation: Upgrade to Next.js >=14.2.10 (or 15+).

rules.title: Express below patched body parser
rules.description: Express 4.x before 4.20.0 ships a body-parser vulnerable to DoS via large bodies.
rules.recommendation: Upgrade Express to >=4.20.0 (or migrate to v5).

rules.title: jsonwebtoken <9 has known signature confusion
rules.description: jsonwebtoken 8.x is affected by signature confusion / weak verify defaults.
rules.recommendation: Upgrade to jsonwebtoken >=9.0.0 and pin algorithm explicitly on verify().

rules.title: lodash below prototype-pollution fix
rules.description: lodash <4.17.21 is vulnerable to prototype pollution.
rules.recommendation: Upgrade lodash to >=4.17.21.

rules.title: node-tar below path-traversal fix
rules.description: tar <6.2.1 is vulnerable to path traversal during extraction.
rules.recommendation: Upgrade tar to >=6.2.1.

rules.title: Generic SECRET-named env variable
rules.description: An env variable simply called SECRET / API_KEY is hard to audit.
rules.recommendation: Rename to a vendor-prefixed name describing its scope.

rules.title: tsconfig strict is disabled
rules.description: Without strict mode, TypeScript permits implicit any and lax null checks.
rules.recommendation: Enable strict:true in tsconfig.json compilerOptions; fix surfaced errors.

rules.title: package.json private not set
rules.description: Without private:true, an accidental npm publish can leak proprietary code.
rules.recommendation: Set private:true in package.json unless meant for publication.
